query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
(630004, 'Who wrote the Acts of the Apostles?', 'multiple_choice', '["Luke", "Paul", "Peter", "John"]', 'Luke', 1),
(630004, 'What event marks the beginning of the church in Acts?', 'multiple_choice', '["The Day of Pentecost", "Jesus'' ascension", "The Jerusalem Council", "Paul''s conversion"]', 'The Day of Pentecost', 2),
(630004, 'According to Acts 1:8, in what order would the Gospel spread?', 'multiple_choice', '["Jerusalem, Judea, Samaria, ends of the earth", "Rome, Greece, Asia, Jerusalem", "Samaria, Jerusalem, Rome, ends of the earth", "Judea, Jerusalem, Samaria, Rome"]', 'Jerusalem, Judea, Samaria, ends of the earth', 3),
(630004, 'How many people were converted after Peter''s sermon at Pentecost?', 'multiple_choice', '["Approximately 3,000", "Approximately 500", "Approximately 5,000", "Approximately 120"]', 'Approximately 3,000', 4),
(630004, 'Who dominates the first half of Acts?', 'multiple_choice', '["Peter", "Paul", "James", "John"]', 'Peter', 5),
(630004, 'What significant event is recorded in Acts 9?', 'multiple_choice', '["Paul''s conversion", "Peter''s vision", "The Jerusalem Council", "Stephen''s martyrdom"]', 'Paul''s conversion', 6),
(630004, 'How many missionary journeys did Paul conduct?', 'multiple_choice', '["Three", "Two", "Four", "Five"]', 'Three', 7),
(630004, 'What was decided at the Jerusalem Council (Acts 15)?', 'multiple_choice', '["Gentiles need not be circumcised to be saved", "All believers must keep the Mosaic Law", "Paul should stop preaching to Gentiles", "The church should remain in Jerusalem"]', 'Gentiles need not be circumcised to be saved', 8),
(630004, 'Who opened the door to Gentile conversion through Cornelius?', 'multiple_choice', '["Peter", "Paul", "Philip", "James"]', 'Peter', 9),
(630004, 'Where does the book of Acts end?', 'multiple_choice', '["Rome", "Jerusalem", "Antioch", "Ephesus"]', 'Rome', 10),
(630004, 'Describe the role of the Holy Spirit in the expansion of the early church as recorded in Acts.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'The Holy Spirit empowered believers at Pentecost, guided the church''s decisions, directed missionary efforts, and enabled bold proclamation of the Gospel.', 11);
command:
mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2KiCrCqNDFcXt7P.577d1a63eeb5 --database P2Dpw9MQsmxzdUQCfGjV6b --execute INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES

(630004, 'How many people were converted after Peter''s sermon at Pentecost?', 'multiple_choice', '["Approximately 3,000", "Approximately 500", "Approximately 5,000", "Approximately 120"]', 'Approximately 3,000', 4),
(630004, 'Who dominates the first half of Acts?', 'multiple_choice', '["Peter", "Paul", "James", "John"]', 'Peter', 5),
(630004, 'What significant event is recorded in Acts 9?', 'multiple_choice', '["Paul''s conversion", "Peter''s vision", "The Jerusalem Council", "Stephen''s martyrdom"]', 'Paul''s conversion', 6),
(630004, 'How many missionary journeys did Paul conduct?', 'multiple_choice', '["Three", "Two", "Four", "Five"]', 'Three', 7),
(630004, 'What was decided at the Jerusalem Council (Acts 15)?', 'multiple_choice', '["Gentiles need not be circumcised to be saved", "All believers must keep the Mosaic Law", "Paul should stop preaching to Gentiles", "The church should remain in Jerusalem"]', 'Gentiles need not be circumcised to be saved', 8),
(630004, 'Who opened the door to Gentile conversion through Cornelius?', 'multiple_choice', '["Peter", "Paul", "Philip", "James"]', 'Peter', 9),
(630004, 'Where does the book of Acts end?', 'multiple_choice', '["Rome", "Jerusalem", "Antioch", "Ephesus"]', 'Rome', 10),
(630004, 'Describe the role of the Holy Spirit in the expansion of the early church as recorded in Acts.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'The Holy Spirit empowered believers at Pentecost, guided the church''s decisions, directed missionary efforts, and enabled bold proclamation of the Gospel.', 11);
